data.narrative:
AQT is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Rainer Blatt, Thomas Monz, and Peter Zoller. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 5 same-sector peers exist in quantum computing, the narrow filters—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yield fewer than 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of quantum computing company maturity, technology approach, and capital efficiency across the peer set. No triangulated valuation range can be derived from the available data without risking spurious precision.

The analysis reveals a fundamental constraint: the quantum computing sector remains sufficiently nascent and dispersed that stage-specific comps are sparse. A credible valuation would require either (1) direct market discovery through process, (2) adoption of fundamentals-based methods (burn rate, runway, IP quality), or (3) deferral pending additional rounds that anchor relative positioning within the peer set. Current data supports none of these paths.
